definition: A single dollar factor (PC1) explains 50-70% of G10 FX variance. PC2 (carry) explains 15-20%, PC3 (momentum) 8-12%. A manager running 9 G10 carry pairs has ~2-3 independent risk factors, not 9. Stress-period dimensionality collapse (correlations spike to 0.85+) further reduces effective diversification when it matters most.
evidence: Stress-period correlations spike to 0.85+ across G10 FX pairs
Naive diversification across 9 G10 pairs dramatically understates risk concentration
Near-mechanical result from covariance decomposition, robust across samples, methodologies, and numeraire choices
August 2024: pairwise correlations among G10 carry positions spiked to 0.85+, collapsing effective dimensionality to ~1.5
Position sizing based on pair count overstates diversification by 3-4x
open_questions: Does the PCA structure help predict the dollar, or is it purely a risk-management tool?
How stable are the PC loadings across different monetary policy regimes?
Whether factor-based risk budgeting can be implemented in real-time given estimation uncertainty during transitions
